insights: New index.html rebuild session started. Prior brain (GS 3.0 15-step) preserved in workflow_history but execution_state reset for Steps 1-12 of v3.2 build.
decisions: Preserved existing public/index.html as starting reference. Will overwrite with new architecture.
issues_found: Existing index.html uses Tailwind CDN + Arabic RTL — new brief requires vanilla CSS + English LTR + 3-dot nav.

insights: Full-screen nav overlay with backdrop blur. 3-dot button morphs to X on open via CSS transforms. Escape key and backdrop click both close the menu.
decisions: Used a single nav-overlay div over a traditional nav bar — cleaner, more immersive, consistent with the minimal aesthetic.
ui_upgrades: Services submenu uses max-height transition for smooth accordion open/close without JS height calculation.

insights: All colors are CSS custom properties. No hardcoded hex values outside :root. Two breakpoints: 640px (mobile) and 1024px (tablet).
decisions: Added --radius-sm/md/lg/xl tokens for consistent border-radius across components. Added --transition shorthand for uniform easing.

insights: Orb uses CSS keyframe animation with scale + translate combo to feel alive. Gradient headline uses background-clip: text for the white-to-glow effect.
decisions: Used clamp() for headline font-size to ensure smooth scaling between mobile and desktop without media query breakpoints for typography.
ui_upgrades: Added pulsing dot inside hero badge to signal live/active state.

insights: Alternating layout achieved via direction:rtl on .service-card.reverse with direction:ltr reset on children — avoids complex CSS grid reordering.
decisions: Used direction flip rather than order reversal to keep HTML in reading order for accessibility and screen readers.

insights: Simulator preview box is an <a> tag wrapping the screen mockup — semantically correct for click-to-navigate. Scanline CSS effect adds realism to the preview.
decisions: Used an <a> element instead of div+onclick for the simulator preview for better keyboard navigation and SEO.
ui_upgrades: Added subtle scanline overlay effect to simulator preview screen using repeating-linear-gradient for visual depth.

insights: Services 2-7 use emoji placeholders in styled containers. All WhatsApp CTAs use wa.me/966570733834. Real estate and Growth CTAs route to ghost-room.html.
decisions: Kept service descriptions to 2-3 sentences per brief instructions. Used HTML entities for special characters to avoid encoding issues.

insights: Both simulator preview cards are <a> links to /growth/ghost-room.html. Hover lifts cards 6px with shadow to signal clickability.
decisions: Kept simulator section as a pure teaser — no embedded chat. Matches brief: this page is a gateway, ghost-room.html is the experience.

insights: System card elevated via translateY(-8px) and subtle teal glow — no badges. Setup fee for System tier displayed in sand-colored callout box for visibility.
decisions: No 'Most Popular' badge anywhere. Elevation and border glow communicate value without manipulation.

insights: Decorative quotation mark is position:absolute, z-index:0, opacity:0.15. Body text at 1.15rem / 1.85 line-height for comfortable reading. First-person, conversational tone.
decisions: Used Georgia serif for signature and quote mark to contrast with Space Grotesk body — adds warmth and human feel.

insights: WhatsApp button is full-width, solid teal in footer — most prominent element. Footer darker than body (#080C18) for visual separation.
decisions: 3-column grid: brand (2fr) | links (1fr) | contact (1.5fr). Brand collapses to full-width on tablet breakpoint.
ui_upgrades: Added 'All systems operational' status indicator with green pulsing dot in footer bottom bar.

insights: All animations trigger at 80% viewport. Service cards: y:60→0 + fade. Pricing + simulator cards: y:40→0 + fade with stagger. Jake's letter: opacity only, no movement. Hero: no animation.
decisions: Used window load event instead of DOMContentLoaded to ensure GSAP scripts (defer) are loaded before registration.
ui_upgrades: once:true on all ScrollTriggers — animations fire once and don't reverse on scroll up. Feels more polished, less distracting.

fixes_applied: Used HTML entities throughout for special characters (em-dash, arrows, curly quotes) to prevent encoding issues
All CTAs minimum 44px height for mobile tap targets
All images/emojis have aria-label or aria-hidden
container padding 1.5rem on mobile prevents text touching screen edges
No banned words (guaranteed, revolutionary, Buy Now) present
No fake badges anywhere in pricing or services
Both USD and SAR shown on all priced tiers
Setup fee clearly called out in Tier 2 pricing

next_action.instruction: Deploy to Render via git push

decisions.decision: WhatsApp number corrected to +966570733834 per prompt (brief uses this number, old site used 96657436016). Using the number from the build prompt.
decisions.reasoning: Build prompt explicitly states wa.me/966570733834 in multiple places.

decisions.decision: Building full 12-step sequence in one pass for efficiency, updating brain atomically after all steps.
decisions.reasoning: All steps produce one deliverable file (public/index.html). Splitting into 12 separate file writes would be inefficient and risk partial states.

decisions.decision: Alternating service card layout implemented via CSS direction:rtl on .reverse class rather than CSS grid order manipulation.
decisions.reasoning: direction flip preserves DOM reading order for screen readers and avoids reordering visual content unpredictably across breakpoints.

decisions.decision: System pricing tier elevated via translateY and border-glow only — zero badges, zero urgency labels.
decisions.reasoning: Brief explicitly bans fake badges. Visual elevation communicates priority without manipulation.

decisions.decision: GSAP scripts use defer attribute and are initialized on window load event, not DOMContentLoaded.
decisions.reasoning: Deferred scripts may not be available at DOMContentLoaded. window load guarantees GSAP and ScrollTrigger are registered before animation setup runs.

insights: The sim-preview component in the AI Receptionist card is the highest-value visual on the page — teal border + scanline effect creates a screen-like feel without actual assets.
direction:rtl trick for alternating layouts breaks down predictably on mobile (640px) by simply removing it — clean fallback.
Using HTML entities for special characters (em-dash, arrows, quotes) is more robust than raw Unicode in HTML files across different editors and deployment pipelines.
